verses.sanskrit: देवतानाम् वचः श्रुत्वा भगवान् वै पितामहः ।प्रत्युवाच सुसंत्रस्तान् कृतान्त बल मोहितान् ॥१-४०-१॥
verses.text:
"On hearing the words of gods, the esteemed Forefather Brahma spoke 
			to them, who are very highly scared, and bewildered by the might of 
			Sagara's sons for they are effectuating the end of all beings." Vishvamitra 
			thus continued his narration. [1-40-1]

verses.sanskrit: यस्य इयम् वसुधा कृत्स्ना वासुदेवस्य धीमतः।महिषी माधवसय स एषा स एव भगवन् प्रभुः॥१-४०-२॥
verses.text:
To whom this Mother Earth belongs in all her entirety, he is that 
			prescient Vasudeva, and she is also the consort of that Maadhava, and 
			that Vishnu eternally props up Mother Earth. Hence, that reverential 
			Vishnu donning the semblance of Sage Kapila will burn down the sons 
			of emperor Sagara to ashes in a fire of fury. [1-40-2, 3]

verses.sanskrit: कापिलम् रूपम् आस्थाय धारयत्य अनिशम् धराम् ।तस्य कोपाग्निना दग्धा भविष्यंति नृपात्मजा ॥१-४०-३॥
पृथिव्याः च अपि निर्भेदो दृष्ट एव सनातनः ।सगरस्य च पुत्राणाम् विनाशो दीर्घ दर्शिनाम् ॥१-४०-४॥
verses.text:
'Even the complete cleavage of the earth, and the complete ruination 
			of Sagara's sons as well, are envisaged by the providentially farsighted 
			ancients." So said Brahma to gods. [1-40-4]

verses.sanskrit: पितामह वचः श्रुत्वा त्रयः त्रिंशत् अरिन्दमः ।देवाः परम संहृष्टाः पुनर् जग्मुर् यथा आगतम् ॥१-४०-५॥
verses.text:
On hearing the words of the Forefather Brahma all the thirty three 
			gods went away with utmost satisfaction, as they have came. [1-40-5]

verses.sanskrit: सगरस्य च पुत्राणाम् प्रादुर् आसीत् महास्वनः ।पृथिव्याम् भिद्यमानायाम् निर्घात सम निःवनः ॥१-४०-६॥
verses.text:
When the sons of Sagara are digging the earth then there emerged an 
			unbearable noise similar to the crashing of a thunder. [1-40-6]

verses.sanskrit: ततो भित्त्वा महीम् सर्वाम् कृत्वा च अपि प्रदक्षिणम् ।सहिताः सगराः सर्वे पितरम् वाक्यम् अब्रुवन् ॥१-४०-७॥
verses.text:
Then on hollowing the entire earth, and on making trips around her 
			without finding the horse, all of the sons of Sagara have come together 
			and on going to their father they spoke these words to him. [1-40-7]

verses.sanskrit: परिक्रांता मही सर्वा सत्त्ववन्तः च सूदिताः ।देव दानव रक्षांसि पिशाच उरग पन्नगाः ॥१-४०-८॥
verses.text:
'Entire earth is trekked and mighty beings like gods, demons, monsters, 
			evil-spirits, fiends, serpents, naga-s are also eliminated, but we have 
			not seen the ritual-horse or its stealer. What we have to do next. Let 
			a thought be given in this matter. Let safeness betide you.' So said 
			those princes to their father Sagara. [1-40-8, 9]

verses.sanskrit: न च पश्यामहे अश्वम् ते अश्व हर्तारम् एव च ।किम् करिष्याम भद्रम् ते बुद्धिः अत्र विचार्यताम् ॥१-४०-९॥
तेषाम् तत् वचनम् श्रुत्वा पुत्राणाम् राज सत्तमः ।समन्युः अब्रवीत् वाक्यम् सगरो रघुनंदन ॥१-४०-१०॥
verses.text:
On hearing that sentence of his sons, oh, Rama, descendent of Raghu, 
			that exalted king Sagara infuriately spoke these words. [1-40-10]

verses.sanskrit: भूयः खनत भद्रम् वो विभेद्य वसुधा तलम्अश्व हर्तारम् आसाद्य कृतार्थाः च निवर्तत ॥१-४०-११॥
verses.text:
'Let the earth be dug further, may safety be with you, and verily 
			splitting open the surface of the earth you catch hold of the thief 
			of the horse, and on achieving your purpose you may return to me.' In 
			this way Sagara ordered his sons. [1-40-11]

verses.sanskrit: पितुर् वचनम् आसाद्य सगरस्य महात्मनः ।षष्टिः पुत्र सहस्राणि रसातलम् अभिद्रवन् ॥१-४०-१२॥
verses.text:
On catching up the words of their father, the great-souled Sagara, 
            his sixty thousand sons rushed towards the surface of the rasaatala , 
            the netherworld. [1-40-12]

verses.sanskrit: खन्यमाने ततः तस्मिन् ददृशुः पर्वत उपमम् ।दिशा गजम् विरूपाक्षम् धारयंतम् महीतलम् ॥१-४०-१३॥
verses.text:
While digging the earth there they beheld a mountain similar easterly 
			elephant named Viruupaaksha, which is bearing the eastern side of earth's 
			surface on its head. [1-40-13]

verses.sanskrit: स पर्वत वनाम् कृत्स्नाम् पृथिवीम् रघुनंदन ।धारयामास शिरसा विरूपाक्षो महागजः ॥१-४०-१४॥
verses.text:
Oh, Rama, the legatee of Raghu, that great easterly elephant Viruupaaksha 
			is bearing the entire eastern earth along with its mountains and forests 
			on its head. [1-40-14]

verses.sanskrit: यदा पर्वणि काकुत्स्थ विश्रमार्थम् महागजः ।खेदात् चालयते शीर्षम् भूमि कम्पः तदा भवेत् ॥१-४०-१५॥
verses.text:
On certain occasions, oh, Rama of Kakutstha, when that great-elephant 
			moves its head desiring respite in tiresomeness then earthquakes will 
			occur on earth. [1-40-15]

verses.sanskrit: ते तम् प्रदक्षिणम् कृत्वा दिशा पालम् महागजम् ।मानयन्तो हि ते राम जग्मुर् भित्त्वा रसातलम् ॥१-४०-१६॥
verses.text:
They circumambulated the great elephant in supplication, oh Rama, and 
			on worshipping that elephant which is the protector of easterly direction 
			they proceeded to netherworld duly splitting the earth. [1-40-16]

verses.sanskrit: ततः पूर्वाम् दिशम् भित्त्वा दक्षिणाम् बिभिदुः पुनः ।दक्षिणस्याम् अपि दिशि ददृशुः ते महागजम् ॥१-४०-१७॥
verses.text:
After splitting the eastern direction then they split opened the southern 
			direction, and even in southern direction they beheld an elephant that 
			is similar to a very great mountain, and that is sustaining southerly 
			side of earth on its head, and on seeing that mammoth elephant of venerable 
			character, namely Mahapadma, they went into an inordinate astonishment. 
			[1-40-17, 18]

verses.sanskrit: महा पद्मम् महात्मानम् सुमहा पर्वतोपमम् ।शिरसा धारयंतम् गाम् विस्मयम् जग्मुर् उत्तमम् ॥१-४०-१८॥
ते तम् प्रदक्षिणम् कृत्वा सगरस्य महात्मनः ।षष्टिः पुत्र सहस्राणि पश्चिमाम् बिभिदुर् दिशम् ॥१-४०-१९॥
verses.text:
On circumabulating that elephant Mahaapada in supplication, they the 
			sixty thousand sons of great-souled Sagara scooped out the westerly 
			direction. [1-40-19]

verses.sanskrit: पश्चिमायाम् अपि दिशि महांतम् अचलोपमम् ।दिशा गजम् सौमनसम् ददृशुः ते महा बलाः ॥१-४०-२०॥
verses.text:
Even in the westerly direction those great mighty sons of Sagara beheld 
			an infinite and mountain similar elephant of western direction, called 
			Sumanasa. [1-40-20]

verses.sanskrit: ते तम् प्रदक्षिणम् कृत्वा पृष्ट्वा च अपि निरामयम् ।खनंतः समुपक्रांता दिशम् सोमवतीम् तदा ॥१-४०-२१॥
verses.text:
Circumambulating that elephant namely Sumanasa in western side, and 
			even on asking about its well-being, then they reached the northern 
			direction duly digging the earth. [1-40-21]

verses.sanskrit: उत्तरस्याम् रघुश्रेष्ठ ददृशुर् हिम पाण्डुरम् ।भद्रम् भद्रेण वपुषा धारयंतम् महीम् इमाम् ॥१-४०-२२॥
verses.text:
In the northern direction, oh, Rama, they beheld Bhadra, a snow-white 
			elephant with an auspicious body bearing northern side of this earth. 
			[1-40-22]

verses.sanskrit: समालभ्य ततः सर्वे कृत्वा च एनम् प्रदक्षिणम् ।षष्टिः पुत्र सहस्राणि बिभिदुर् वसुधा तलम् ॥१-४०-२३॥
verses.text:
On touching that elephant reverently and also circumambulating it, 
			those sixty thousands sons of Sagara further burrowed to the surface 
			of the earth. [1-40-23]

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्राक् उत्तराम् गत्वा सागराः प्रथिताम् दिशम् ।रोषात् अभ्यखनन् सर्वे पृथिवीम् सगर आत्मजाः ॥१-४०-२४॥
verses.text:
The sons of Sagara have then gone to the auspicious northeast direction, iishaana-digbhaaga, Shiva-sthaana , and 
			all those sons of Sagara have tunnelled the earth rancorously. [1-40-24]

verses.sanskrit: ते तु सर्वे महत्मानो भिमवेग महबलाः ।ददृशुः कपिलम् तत्र वासुदेवम् सनातनम् ॥१-४०-२५॥
verses.text:
But all those great-souled and great-mighty ones with terrible dash 
			have seen the Infinite Vasudeva in the form of sage Kapila there in 
			the northeast, and oh, descendant of Raghu, they have also seen the 
			ritual-horse moving nearby that sage Kapila, thus all of the sons of 
			Sagara obtained a matchless delight. [1-40-25, 26]

verses.sanskrit: हयम् च तस्य देवस्य चरन्तम् अविदूरतः ।प्रहर्षम् अतुलम् प्रप्तः सर्वे ते रघुनंदन ॥१-४०-२६॥
ते तम् हय हरम् ज्ञात्वा क्रोध पर्याकुल ईक्षणाः ।खनित्र लांगला धर नाना वृक्ष शिला धराः ॥१-४०-२७॥
verses.text:
The eyes of the princes are agitated in fury in construing the sage 
			Kapila as the stealer of ritual-horse, and they furiously dashed towards 
			him wielding crowbars, ploughs, and various trees and boulders, and 
			shouting at him, 'stay, stay.' [1-40-27, 28a]

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यधावन्त संक्रुद्धाः तिष्ठ तिष्ठ इति च अब्रुवन् ।अस्माकम् त्वम् हि तुरगम् यज्ञियम् हृतवान् असि ॥१-४०-२८॥
verses.text:
You the malicious one, you have indeed stolen our ritual-horse, and 
			in fact, you should know that we who arrived here are the sons of Sagara.' 
			Thus Sagara's sons shouted at sage Kapila. [1-40-28b, 29a]

verses.sanskrit: दुर्मेधः त्वम् हि संप्राप्तान् विद्धि नः सगरात्मजान् ।श्रुत्वा तत् वचनम् तेषाम् कपिलो रघुनंदन ॥१-४०-२९॥
verses.text:
On hearing their words, oh, Rama, the descendant of Raghu, then sage 
			Kapila is beset with high wrath, and he boomed a 'hum' sound at them. 
			[1-40-29b, 30a]

verses.sanskrit: रोषेण महता आविष्टो हुम् कारम् अकरोत् तदा ।ततः तेन अप्रमेयेण कपिलेन महात्मना ।भस्म राशी कृताः सर्वे काकुत्स्थ सगरात्मजाः ॥१-४०-३०॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama of Kakutstha, then that great-souled sage Kapila, rather Vishnu 
			with unimaginable power, has rendered all of those sons of Sagara as 
			heaps of ashes by virtue of his 'hum' sound." Thus Sage Vishvamitra 
			is narrating the legend of Sagara to Rama and others. [1-40-30b, c]
